book_summary: Numbers is complete across all 36 chapters in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The completed book follows Israel from Sinai census and camp order through wilderness breakdown, shared leadership, land scouting, priestly disputes, death and succession, Balaam's transformed blessings, second-generation census, women's inheritance petitions, sacred-time calendars, patriarchal vow law, the morally catastrophic Midian war, Transjordan negotiation, journey memory, land boundaries, refuge cities, and the final Zelophehad inheritance ruling. The dataset preserves difficult source material while explicitly rejecting militarization, colonialism, genocide, child killing, enslavement, sexual violence, misogyny, racism, xenophobia, antisemitism, supersessionism, zealot violence, clerical authoritarianism, unsafe asceticism, capital punishment, vigilantism, and modern territorial weaponization.

chapters.notes: Second census, generational transition, Korah's surviving sons, named daughters, proportional land allocation, and separate Levite inheritance structure.
Daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly and change inheritance law; Moses' Meribah accountability and public commissioning of Joshua.
Daily, Sabbath, new-moon, Passover/Unleavened Bread, and Weeks sanctuary offerings.
Seventh-month blast day, Day of Atonement, Sukkot descending offerings, and eighth-day assembly.
Vow obligations, father/husband nullification authority, widowed/divorced women's autonomy, and explicit patriarchal asymmetry.
Midian vengeance war, child and female-captive killing commands, retained girls, corpse purification, human spoil division, and categorical anti-genocide moral protest.
Reuben/Gad settlement negotiation, armed-vanguard obligation, family settlements, east-Jordan allocation, conquest, rebuilding, and renaming.
Wilderness itinerary, Aaron's dated death, plains-of-Moab endpoint, and conquest/dispossession commands under anti-colonial safeguards.
Ancient Canaan boundary map, east-Jordan exception, and shared priestly/civic/tribal land-allocation council.
Levite cities, six cities of refuge, intent and evidence rules, blood-avenger limits, one-witness prohibition, and anti-death-penalty safeguards.
Zelophehad daughters' inheritance preserved through clan-marriage restriction, with agency and patriarchal land control held in tension.

translation_notes: The second census records generational transition, not human worth by military capacity, male lineage, fertility, tribe, or number.
Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah speak publicly, are declared right by YHWH, and cause inheritance law to expand.
Joshua's commissioning is public, non-dynastic, spirit-centered, and accountable to priestly discernment.
Numbers 28-29 preserves Israel's sanctuary calendar and living Jewish sacred times without Christian supersession or private sacrifice reenactment.
Numbers 30 preserves an explicitly patriarchal vow structure under critique; modern reception must center equal agency and informed consent.
Numbers 31 is morally catastrophic war material: child killing, captive girls, human beings counted as spoil, and plunder may never be normalized, reenacted, or called timeless ethics.
Numbers 32's accountable negotiation does not erase its conquest, dispossession, and renaming of inhabited land.
Numbers 33's itinerary can serve communal memory, while its final dispossession and iconoclasm commands remain categorically blocked from modern use.
Numbers 34's ancient boundary map is not a modern deed, state border, or permission to displace Palestinians, Israelis, Indigenous peoples, or any living population.

Numbers 35's cities of refuge interrupt immediate revenge through asylum, witnesses, intent analysis, and communal judgment; its execution provisions do not authorize capital punishment or vigilantism.
Numbers ends with women whose legal courage changed Torah, even as the final tribal-property ruling constrains their marriage choices.
Mystical companion layers remain interpretive and never replace restored/source-audited text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Numbers 26 clan names and census totals require textual-witness and arithmetic audit.
Numbers 26:11 must crosslink with Numbers 16 household-death language and Korahite survival traditions.
Numbers 27 daughters-of-Zelophehad law requires gender, property, and later Jewish legal-reception audit.
Numbers 27 Joshua commissioning requires succession, Urim, and Deuteronomy/Joshua crosslinks.
Numbers 28-29 offering quantities require arithmetic, calendar, Leviticus 23, and later Jewish liturgical audit.
Numbers 30 verse numbering and vow terminology require Hebrew/English versification and patriarchal-household-law audit.
Numbers 31 Midian war requires maximum anti-genocide, anti-enslavement, anti-sexual-violence, child-protection, and historical-source safeguards.
Numbers 31 captive-girl language and human tribute require explicit trauma-informed reception analysis.
Numbers 32 east-Jordan settlement and city renaming require territorial, archaeological, and anti-colonial audit.
Numbers 33 itinerary requires geography, source-layer, and travel-list comparison across Torah.
Numbers 33 dispossession and sacred-site destruction require categorical anti-colonial and anti-iconoclasm metadata.
Numbers 34 place identifications and idealized borders require geography and modern non-weaponization audit.
Numbers 35 city measurements, blood-avenger law, high-priest release, and witness rules require legal-history and anti-capital-punishment audit.
Numbers 36 endogamy ruling requires gender-agency, tribal-property, Jubilee, and anti-coercion audit.
next_actions: Begin Deuteronomy in a genuinely large batch, starting with chapters 1-11: Moses' historical retelling, leadership distribution, wilderness failures, conquest memories, Shema, covenant love, land theology, and warnings against forgetting—with anti-colonial, anti-authoritarian, anti-antisemitism, and anti-weaponization safeguards.
